factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, driven by e-commerce and omni-channel retail sales.

evidence.text: Given these trends, our products most aligned with business-to-consumer shipments have experienced the strongest growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to e-commerce and omni-channel sales growth.

factors.summary: The company has successfully raised prices, but revenue per piece has been mixed due to lower fuel surcharge rates and customer mix changes.

evidence.text: Revenue per piece for ground and air products was positively impacted by a base rate increase on December 28, 2015. UPS Ground rates and accessorial charges increased an average net 4.9%, while UPS Air services and accessorial charges increased an average net 5.2%. The surcharge increased for Over Maximum Packages and the index tables for the Ground and Air fuel surcharges were adjusted effective November 2, 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raised prices despite mixed revenue per piece results.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward premium products, which is beneficial.

evidence.text: The continuing shift in product mix as the growth in premium products continues to exceed the growth in our standard products.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting towards premium products, which is beneficial.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by volume increases and rate hikes.

evidence.text: Volume increased in the third quarter and year-to-date periods of 2016 compared with 2015, despite having one less operating day, primarily due to continued growth in the retail, healthcare, automotive and professional services segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating due to volume increases and rate hikes.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across both U.S. and international segments, driven by e-commerce and business-to-consumer shipments.

evidence.text: Average Daily Package Volume increased in both U.S. Domestic and International operations, with strong growth in e-commerce and business-to-consumer shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across both U.S. and international segments.

factors.summary: Healthcare, automotive, and professional services are growing fastest in the U.S., while international growth is strong in Europe and Asia.

evidence.text: The increase in domestic volume in the third quarter and year-to-date periods of 2016 was primarily due to growth in Italy, France, Turkey and Canada. The export volume growth in the third quarter and year-to-date periods of 2016 was mainly driven by our European and Asian operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in key sectors and international regions.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are declining, but labor costs are increasing.

evidence.text: Total domestic fuel surcharge revenue decreased by $40 million in the third quarter of 2016 ($241 million year-to-date) as a result of lower fuel surcharge rates caused by declining jet and diesel fuel prices. Higher pension and healthcare costs, contractual union wage increases and higher workers' compensation adjustments in 2015 more than offset the volume growth, productivity improvements and net effect of fuel (fuel expense decreased faster than fuel surcharge revenue).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed impact from declining fuel costs and increasing labor costs.

factors.summary: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to higher wages, health and welfare costs, and workers' compensation adjustments.

evidence.text: Higher employee compensation expenses, which were impacted by (1) an increase in average daily union labor hours to support volume growth; (2) an increase in wage rates and (3) an increase in health and welfare costs (due to headcount and contractual contribution rate increases to multiemployer plans). For the third quarter of 2016, these costs include an increase in workers' compensation expense due to more favorable actuarial adjustments in 2015 compared with 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, causing margin pressure.

factors.summary: Operating margins are stable or slightly decreasing due to cost increases and lower fuel surcharge rates.

evidence.text: Operating margin decreased 70 basis points to 13.5% for the third quarter of 2016. On a year-to-date basis, operating margin decreased 10 basis points to 13.1%. The impact of lower fuel prices was partially mitigated by pricing changes to the fuel surcharge indices, as well as the overall increase in package volume.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are decreasing due to cost increases and lower fuel surcharge rates.

factors.summary: Cost actions, including network adjustments and technology improvements, are helping to manage expenses.

evidence.text: We continued to adjust our air capacity and cost structure in our transportation network to better match the prevailing volume levels. The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost actions like network adjustments and technology improvements are helping manage expenses.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to technology initiatives and network adjustments.

evidence.text: Productivity improvements have continued to be realized through adjusting our air and ground networks to better match volume levels and utilizing technology to increase package sorting and delivery efficiency. The continued deployment of ORION has contained the growth of average daily vehicle miles driven, while the increased redirect of SurePost volume to optimize delivery density on UPS vehicles has reduced the delivery costs for business-to-consumer shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving due to technology initiatives and network adjustments.

factors.summary: The supply chain is adapting well to changing demand patterns.

evidence.text: Our broad portfolio of product offerings and the flexibilities inherent in our transportation network have helped us adapt to these changing trends.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The supply chain is adapting well to changing demand patterns.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is being adjusted to match demand, with cautious expansion.

evidence.text: We continued to adjust our air capacity and cost structure in our transportation network to better match the prevailing volume levels.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capacity utilization is being adjusted to match demand, with cautious expansion.

factors.summary: The company is investing in technology and facilities to improve efficiency and capacity.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability. Additionally, we have continued to utilize newly expanded operating facilities to improve time-in-transit for shipments in each region.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is investing in technology and facilities to improve efficiency and capacity.

factors.summary: The economic environment is mixed, with modest GDP growth and some industrial weakness, but overall growth is present.

evidence.text: The U.S. economic environment has continued to be mixed as relatively stable consumer conditions are somewhat offset by continued weakness in industrial production, soft business investment and higher inventory levels. We continue to see modest GDP growth and U.S. manufacturing has shown some positive signs of growth in recent months, but remains weak overall and continues to hinder the pace of expansion in the overall small package delivery market.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The economic environment is mixed with modest growth and some industrial weakness.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are affecting international results, particularly in revenue and operating expenses.

evidence.text: Currency Benefit / (Cost) – (in millions)*:                 Revenue         $ (26 )           $ (100 ) Operating Expenses         19           93 Operating Profit         $ (7 )           $ (7 ) * Net of currency hedging; amount represents the change in currency translation compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are negatively affecting international results.

factors.summary: The impending exit of the United Kingdom from the European Union creates some uncertainty regarding its impact on global GDP.

evidence.text: The impending exit of the United Kingdom from the European Union creates some uncertainty regarding its impact on global GDP.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The uncertainty regarding the UK's exit from the EU poses a moderate risk to global GDP.

factors.summary: Management is confident in the company's initiatives, particularly in technology deployment and network adjustments.

evidence.text: We believe these adjusted financial measures are important indicators of our recurring operations because they exclude items that may not be indicative of, or are unrelated to, our core operating results, and provide a better baseline for analyzing trends in our underlying businesses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence in technology deployment and network adjustments indicates a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic uncertainties, currency fluctuations, and geopolitical factors like the UK's exit from the EU.

evidence.text: The uneven nature of economic growth worldwide and volatile currency markets have continued shifting trade patterns and weakened demand in certain trade lanes. The impending exit of the United Kingdom from the European Union creates some uncertainty regarding its impact on global GDP.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include economic uncertainties, currency fluctuations, and geopolitical factors, which are expected to impact performance negatively.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on technology, network efficiency, and expanding capabilities.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's clear focus on technology, network efficiency, and expansion suggests significant positive developments in the long term.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, currency fluctuations, and volume demand.

evidence.text: The impact of lower fuel prices was partially mitigated by pricing changes to the fuel surcharge indices, as well as the overall increase in package volume. Currency movements have affected international revenue and operating expenses.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities to fuel prices, currency fluctuations, and volume demand present moderate risks to performance.

factors.summary: New product launches and technology deployments are catalysts for future growth.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of ORION has contained the growth of average daily vehicle miles driven, while the increased redirect of SurePost volume to optimize delivery density on UPS vehicles has reduced the delivery costs for business-to-consumer shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and technology deployments are expected to drive future growth.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include technology, scale, and service differentiation.

evidence.text: Our broad portfolio of product offerings and the flexibilities inherent in our transportation network have helped us adapt to these changing trends.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's competitive advantages in technology, scale, and service differentiation position it favorably in the market.
